course_title: Foundation Studio: Elements of Architectural Design

learning_outcomes.description: Identify key principles and practices that inform architectural design processes.
Select and examine architectural precedents from across culture and periods to recognise concepts of architecture.
Identify the elements of architectural design and their interrelationships.
Identify the elements of sustainable design to integrate into the project.
Creatively translate elements of architectural design to develop an architectural design project.
Use iterative processes to design architectural solutions that address contemporary challenges at a variety of scales and levels of complexity.
Communicate architectural ideas effectively through diagramming, drawings and model making.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale

The Weekly Progress Journal documents your design progress over the course of the semester. In this task, students will document weekly process work (including diagrams, drawings, maquettes and notations) made in response to a set of weekly studio tasks. This may include precedent studies, project visits and studio-based activities. Specific details and requirements will be provided in the studio outline and course handouts on Blackboard ULTRA. Weekly submissions (1% each) are due every Thursday at 9am from Week 2 to Week 12. 

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and/or Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines. Any physical models should be photographed and a minimum of two photographs describing the complete and full model must be included in the digital submission.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale

This assessment involves a design-based project combining the knowledge and skills developed through weekly studio activities. In this project, students will work towards an initial design proposal that combines a suite of individual architectural elements (hearth, enclosure, canopy, platform and screen) to form a Pavilion of Seasons. Weekly studio activities will serve to scaffold the design process. Assessment 2 details, requirements and deliverables are provided in the studio outline on Blackboard ULTRA.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

assessments.due_date:
Digital submission 28/10/2026 4:00 pm

Exhibition and Roundtable Presentation in Class 29/10/2026 1:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale

Assessment 3 involves the continuation of the previous Assessment’s design-based project. Students will iterate upon their initial design proposal for the Pavilion of Seasons using a range of compositional strategies (structure, surface, sensation, threshold and narrative) in order to orchestrate an elemental approach into a more cohesive and unified whole. Weekly studio activities will continue to support the design process, including a Week 13 exhibition and roundtable presentation of submitted work. Assessment 3 details, requirements and deliverables are provided in the studio outline on Blackboard ULTRA.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale

This final assessment involves the production of a self-reflective annotated project markup based upon observations and feedback garnered at the Assessment 3 roundtable presentation. In this task, students will produce a single, red-pen markup of their Assessment 3 sectional drawing, noting and amending any areas that require further development. Assessment 4 details, requirements and deliverables are provided in the studio outline on Blackboard ULTRA.
